title: Gados
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Update|[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]], [[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]}} {{Youmay|the species|the [[Gados language]]}} {{Species |image=[[File:Gados.jpg]] |name=Gados |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]{{CSWECite|I|306}} |class= |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Yellow]] {{C|only for aged Gados}}''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' |haircolor=Light brown,[[Heir to the Empire 4|''Heir to the Empire'' 4]] white |feathers= |eyecolor=Dark,''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' red |distinctions=Prodigious leaping ability, ribbon-like vital organs |lifespan= |origin=[[Abregado-rae/Legends|Abregado-rae]] |habitat= |diet= |language=[[Gados language]], [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] }} {{Quote|We'll be back soon. I just want to check out the Gados's manufacturing infrastructure.|C-3PO, impersonating Leia Organa Solo to Winter|''[[Heir to the Empire]]''}} The '''Gados''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with slender, fur-covered bodies. They had elongated necks and lanky [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], and their heads featured only two large [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and a pair of protruding [[Tooth/Legends|incisors]]. Gados internal organs ran like ribbons throughout their bodies. This arrangement meant that any injury a Gados sustained was potentially critical, with the loss of an appendage almost certainly [[Death/Legends|fatal]]. The species was incredibly nimble; they were known throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for their gymnastic skills, an ability made famous by the [[Leaping Tee]] group of [[Acrobat/Legends|acrobats]] who toured with the [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]] [[Alsakan Circo-Menagerie|Circo-Menagerie]]. The Gados were indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Abregado-rae/Legends|Abregado-rae]] in the [[Abregado system/Legends|Abregado system]] of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. They developed a simple society independent of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld's]] other native sentients, the [[Moocher]]s, and the [[galactic community]] largely left them to their own devices for [[Standard year/Legends|millennia]]. However, their world's relative obscurity and its location at the head of the [[Rimma Trade Route/Legends|Rimma Trade Route]] made it an ideal staging area for [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], and other [[Scoundrel/Legends|scoundrels]]. Gados were known as highly amenable, overlooking major eccentricities and breaches of protocol, tolerance that encouraged immigrants from the galactic fringe to come to Abregado-rae, and the ever-amiable Gados welcomed them and learned the secrets of galactic technology. Over [[Time/Legends|time]], the Gados abandoned most aspects of their native culture, including the [[Gados language]]. As their culture became congruent with the galactic standard, they assimilated into the amalgamated culture of the [[Alien/Legends|alien]] inhabitants of Abregado-rae. After the rise of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], a multi-species oligarchy known as the [[Tundei]] regime took control of Abregado-rae's government and instigated strict new laws that challenged the longstanding frontier nature of the world. Although the Tundei tried to shift the planet's economy from criminal endeavors to manufacturing, conditions on the world worsened, and the Gados suffered. Especially heinous were new punishments for crimes, such as [[Dismemberment/Legends|amputations]] and executions—which, to the Gados, were essentially the same thing. Nevertheless, some Gados supported the Tundei, working as [[Spy/Legends|spies]]. ==Biology and appearance== [[File:Gado_gambler_HTTE_4.jpg|thumb|left|Gados were characterized by shaggy fur, protruding eyes, and buck teeth.]] Gados were tall, slender [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] whose thin frames were covered in shaggy fur. Despite their generally [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] anatomy, a Gados's [[Limb/Legends|arms]] stretched to a greater length than his or her legs. Even longer was the attenuated neck, which stood the typical Gados head and shoulders over an average-sized [[Human/Legends|Human]]. Upon this perched a small, round head that curved forward. The Gados face was simple, featuring only two bulbous [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]—which could be black or red in [[Color/Legends|color]]—and a pair of white buck [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] extending from behind a cleft lip. A Gados's skin turned yellow with age, but this was hidden under a coat of white or light brown fur. Although Gados were not particularly strong or tough, they were incredibly nimble and lithesome. The Gados [[Bone/Legends|skeleton]] contained only small pieces of bone, which were held together by muscle and ligaments. The arrangement permitted their muscles to expend great bursts of energy, which allowed them to leap about acrobatically and to great heights. Such gymnastics did not easily tire the Gados; the same individual could leap and jump, flip, and tumble for long stretches, looking to others almost like dancers.[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' novelization]] Even elderly Gados enjoyed this ability to bounce and somersault with ease. Like the Gados body, the [[Species/Legends|species]]' vital organs were thin and elongated. Rather than being concentrated in a particular area, each organ ran like a ribbon from the Gados's head to his or her feet. This arrangement left Gados vulnerable to injury, as every portion of the body housed some sensitive tissue. Accordingly, amputations and severed limbs were essentially critical blows in most cases. ==Society and culture== {{Quote|This is all very interesting, I'm sure. Some of us would like to play cards, though.|Unidentified Gados gambler|}} [[File:AbregadoRae1319.jpg|thumb|right|upright|Gados interacted with other species, such as Humans and Moochers, on their homeworld.]] Gados were amiable, tolerant beings with the ability to ignore habits, quirks, and character faults that other beings might find annoying or offensive. They were friendly to a fault, greeting strangers openly and warmly. This combination of welcoming nature and tolerant attitude led them to turn a blind eye to criminal activity. Despite their friendliness, their ability to bounce and leap allowed a threatened Gados to roll about quickly and confuse an attacker. Gados were also known for their high level of cultural adaptability. Although a distinct Gados society existed on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] at one point, with its own art, cuisine, and technology,''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]''. [[Standard year/Legends|millennia]] of contact with other species erased most traces of it. For example, the Gados once spoke their own language, also known as [[Gados language|Gados]], but over thousands of years, most Gados turned to [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] as their primary tongue. Over [[Time/Legends|time]], Gados began to dress like other species, use [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]]-standard technology, and adopt affectations such as the smoking of [[Hookah pipe/Legends|hookah pipes]] like other species. After this embrace of galactic culture took root, many Gados became aware of the loss of their indigenous way of life and were saddened by it. Like all citizens of [[Abregado-rae/Legends|Abregado-rae]], Gados were divided into clans.''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' They lived in [[City/Legends|cities]] of canals, serviced by [[Water/Legends|watercraft]] known as [[Gados Floatboat]]s. In these environments, Gados cohabited with cosmopolitan populations of many different species and, beginning after the fall of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], were ruled by a multi-species group known as the [[Tundei]]. Despite the diversity of the city, Gados were charged with maintaining the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Abregado-rae Spaceport|lone spaceport]]. ==History== {{Quote|Well, well. Has civilization finally come to Abregado?''"
"''Stranger things have happened.|Lando Calrissian and Han Solo, upon visiting Abregado-rae after the rise of the Tundei regime|}} The Gados evolved on Abregado-rae, a world of rolling hills and little biodiversity that also served as home to the [[Moocher]] species. Beings from another planet first visited the world between [[15,000 BBY/Legends|15,000]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' and [[10,000 BBY]], but the species was left alone for thousands of years, during which they developed a distinct culture, society, and technology. By [[3000 BBY]], Abregado-rae had become the most [[Deep Core/Legends|Coreward]] stop on the [[Rimma Trade Route/Legends|Rimma Trade Route]], but the planet and its inhabitants were largely ignored by the [[galactic community]]. [[Smuggler/Legends|Smugglers]], [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], and other [[Scoundrel/Legends|scoundrels]], on the other hand, took advantage of the Gados' tolerant natures and began using the planet as an alternate starting point for runs along the Rimma, thus avoiding the stricter law enforcement of the [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]]. With the long-term presence of offworlders on Abregado-rae, the Gados adopted galactic-standard technology and built a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]].{{CSWECite|I|7}} Over time, the Gados abandoned most aspects of their indigenous culture, and a tentative multi-species government was established on the world. Even their spaceport lost its native name, coming to be known as simply "Abregado-rae spaceport." Abregado-rae became known galaxy-wide as a [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]], particularly known as a conduit for [[Spice/Legends|spice]] smuggling. Meanwhile, Gados colonists founded settlements on other planets in the [[Abregado system/Legends|star system]]. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' the species' home [[Star system/Legends|system]] was the site of a [[Battle of Abregado/Legends|battle]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].{{TCW|Rising Malevolence|Rising ''Malevolence''}} After the collapse of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Gados homeworld was claimed as part of [[Zero Command]] under the rule of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Blitzer Harrsk]]. However, by [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], the world was free of Harrsk, and had joined the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] by [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. The world at this time had some forty million inhabitants, sixty percent of whom were Gados. Abregado-rae had by this point come under the rule of the [[Tundei]], a group that overthrew the previous, mostly hands-off government in a coup. The Tundei outlawed piracy and smuggling and hired [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police]] to patrol the streets and arrest lawbreakers. The new rulers tried to replace the criminal activities that had once sustained the economy with industry and manufacturing. They also ordered the spaceport refurbished as a gesture to the commerce they hoped to generate. Despite these changes, the Tundei limited the freedom enjoyed by the Gados in years prior. Crime was prosecuted by the [[Tundei Tribunal]], headed by a [[Herglic/Legends|Herglic]] named [[Shelov]], with punishments consisting of [[Torture/Legends|torture]], amputation, or execution, all of which amounted to the same thing to Gados due to their unique vital organs. Some Gados actively opposed the Tundei; a group of Gados colonists from other planets in the system offered a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on the head of Shelov, for instance. Still, some Gados supported the Tundei; an older Gados named [[Trarr]], for example, manufactured [[Transparisteel/Legends|transparisteel]] but secretly [[Spy/Legends|spied]] for the government. In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], when a group of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] agreed to send a shipment of surplus war rations to a group of peaceful dissidents in the hills outside the city, Trarr reported them to the authorities. Ultimately, the Tundei regime failed to attract the new investment they sought. Nevertheless, the world remained important to the New Republic for its manufacturing. The New Republic liaisons [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] visited the world in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] under the cover story that they were investigating this manufacturing, although their true mission was to make contact with [[Fynn Torve]], a representative of the smuggler [[Talon Karrde]]. The pair found Torve in a [[Casino/Legends|casino]], playing [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] at a table with a [[Unidentified Gados gambler|Gados]] and other beings. Solo joined the game, but his stalling irritated the Gados [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]]. Following the [[Thrawn campaign]] of 9 ABY, the New Republic pressured the Tundei to change their laws, but the regime refused. Years later, the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|Yuuzhan Vong invasion]] of the galaxy flooded Abregado-rae with refugees. The Tundei saw their grip on the world slipping, and the planet seemed on the brink of another government overthrow. The [[Yuuzhan Vong]] eventually captured the world. By [[137 ABY]], Abregado-rae fell within territory claimed by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Krayt]] as part of his [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]]. ==Gados in the galaxy== Although Gados were spread between the various habitable planets of the Abregado system, few members of the species traveled to other parts of the galaxy. One major exception was the [[Leaping Tee]] family, a group of itinerate Gados performers who traveled the galaxy putting on [[Acrobat/Legends|acrobatics]] and tumbling shows as part of the [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]] [[Alsakan Circo-Menagerie|Circo-Menagerie]]. At their height, the Leaping Tee family was extremely popular, and for many citizens of the galactic community, they were the face of the Gados species. ==Behind the scenes== The Gados are mentioned in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s novel ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' ([[1991]]), but the book provides no description of their appearance or culture. The novel uses both the terms "Gado" and "Gados," leaving it unclear what the singular form of the species' name truly is. In the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] [[Star Wars: Heir to the Empire (comic series)|adaptation]] of a scene from ''Heir to the Empire'' ([[1996]]) where Han Solo plays sabacc with a group of gamblers on Abregado-rae, a furred alien is depicted as one of the players, as drawn by [[Olivier Vatine]] and [[Fred Blanchard]]. The species' description in the book ''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' ([[1998]]), by author [[Daniel Wallace]], is based on Vatine and Blanchard's [[Alien/Legends|alien]]. The book ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' ([[2003]]) for [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' expands on the species' history and biology. It also provides statistics for Gados characters. The game represents the species as highly agile, yet not very tough or perceptive. Average members of the species know some sort of profession, have the ability to leap long distances, and are able to tumble like a gymnast. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Gados}} *[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' novelization]] {{Ab|Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] {{Mo}} *''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' {{Ab|Heir to the Empire (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *[[Heir to the Empire 4|''Heir to the Empire'' 4]] {{1stp}} ==Sources== *''[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' *''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War'': Prima Official Game Guide]] *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion'': Prima Official Game Guide]] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{ScrollBox|content={{Reflist}}}} [[Category:Core Worlds sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Gados]] }}
